title: centaurea aspera subsp. scorpiurifolia
intro: Centaurea aspera subsp. scorpiurifolia, also known as Rough Chervil or Rough Cornflower, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves, and a distinctive purple flower. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Centaurea aspera subsp. scorpiurifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
